name: Jill's Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Italian seasoning (or 1 tablespoon oregano and 1 tablespoon basil)
1/2 cup Pine nuts, toasted or raw
Steamed broccoli; spinach; artichokes or sliced mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a slack dough either by hand, electric mixer, or bread machine with the first 9 ingredients.
2. Add the olives and pine nuts just before the last 1/2 cup of flour if hand kneading or using a mixer, or 5 minutes before the end of the bread machine cycle (to keep them whole).
3. Let dough double in size about 1 1/2 hours.
4. Punch down and spread in a cornmeal-dusted, heavily floured 14-inch round pizza pan.
5. Make indentations in the dough with your finger tips and cover; let rise for 45 minutes or until doubled and puffy.
6. Push vegetables of choice into dough to keep them anchored down.
7. Preheat oven to 500°F (250°C), then turn it down to 450°F (230°C).
8. Bake foccacia 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
9. Remove from oven and sprinkle with fresh basil and cracked black pepper. Fresh tomato slices, chopped garlic, and a bit of olive oil enhance the flavor.
